a: Discover how many people subscribe to your broadcast channels, and how many messages they receive.
Download and analyze push-notification metrics.
An object that encapsulates the device token you use to deliver push notifications to your app.
An object that contains information about a received PushKit notification.
An object that requests the delivery and handles the receipt of PushKit notifications.
The methods that you use to handle incoming PushKit notifications and registration events.
Constants reflecting the push types you want to support.
An object that contains metadata about a received PushKit VoIP notification.
Respond to push notifications related to your app’s complications, file providers, and VoIP services.

t: Responding to VoIP Notifications from PushKit
a: Receive incoming Voice-over-IP (VoIP) push notifications and use them to display the system call interface to the user.

t: Supporting PushKit Notifications in Your App
a: Declare the types of PushKit notifications your app supports and configure objects to respond to them.
A push type for watchOS complications.
The delegate object that receives notifications coming from the push registry object.
Registers the push types for this push registry object.
The contents of the received payload.
A push type for file provider updates.
Creates a push registry with the specified dispatch queue.
A boolean indicating whether a VoIP app must report a call or live conversation in response to receiving this VoIP notification.
Tells the delegate that the system invalidated the push token for the specified type.
Notifies the delegate that a remote push has been received.
Tells the delegate that a remote push notification arrived.

The system calls this method when it receives a VoIP push notification. Use this method to extract data from the notification’s payload and to report a VoIP call using CallKit or LiveCommunicationKit.
Tells the delegate that the system updated the credentials for the specified type of push notification.
Retrieves the locally cached push token for the specified push type.
A unique device token to use when sending push notifications to the current device.
The type value indicating how to interpret the payload.
The push type constant associated with the token.
A push type for Voice-over-IP (VoIP) call invitations.
